hooks.prompt:
Check if the edited file is a test file (.test.ts, .test.tsx, .spec.ts, .spec.tsx). If it IS a test file, verify these testing best practices:

1. Tests have descriptive names that explain the expected behavior, not just 'it works' or 'test 1'
2. No hardcoded timeouts like setTimeout(done, 5000) — use proper async/await patterns
3. Each test focuses on ONE behavior — not testing multiple unrelated things
4. Using semantic queries (getByRole, getByLabelText) instead of getByTestId for React component tests
5. No implementation details tested (internal state, private methods, CSS classes) — test behavior and output
6. Mocks are scoped properly — using mockReturnValueOnce over mockReturnValue when the mock should only apply once
7. No console.log left in test files — use proper assertions
8. Async operations use await/waitFor, not arbitrary delays
9. Test data uses factories or builders, not copy-pasted objects across tests
10. Cleanup is handled (afterEach, vi.restoreAllMocks) to prevent test pollution

If it is NOT a test file, respond with {"ok": true}.

If it IS a test file and all checks pass, respond {"ok": true}.
If any check fails, respond {"ok": false, "reason": "Brief description of the testing issue found"}.

$ARGUMENTS
